channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: US Aerial Refuelling Aircraft Crashes in Western Iraq, Second Plane Lands Safely
item.description: According to the US military, an American KC-135 aerial refuelling aircraft has crashed in western Iraq while a second plane involved in the operation managed to land safely. The US Central Command (C...

item.title: How to Make the Most of Travel Loyalty Points Without Losing Value
item.description: In the complex world of travel loyalty programs, hoarding points without a clear strategy often leads to disappointment. Loyalty points can lose value due to 'devaluations,' which occur when programs ...

item.title: Armed man killed after driving truck into Michigan synagogue, no injuries reported
item.description: An armed man drove a truck into the hallway of Temple Israel, a synagogue in West Bloomfield Township near Detroit, Michigan, while children were attending the centre's early childhood program. The ve...

item.title: U.S. KC-135 Refuelling Aircraft Crashes in Western Iraq Amid Military Operations Against Iran
item.description: On 12th March 2026, a U.S. Air Force Boeing KC-135 Stratotanker, used for aerial refuelling, crashed in western Iraq. According to U.S. Central Command, the incident involved another aircraft, but it ...

item.title: Yanga face Tanzania Prisons with top league spot at stake
item.description: Tonight at Jamhuri Stadium, Young Africans S.C. (Yanga) are set to clash with Tanzania Prisons in a crucial Premier League match. Yanga are leading the league comfortably with 32 points from 12 matche...

item.title: SWAN Mourns Passing of NAqF President, Chamberlain Nnamdi Dunkwu
item.description: The Sports Writers Association of Nigeria (SWAN) don express serious condolences to Nigeria Aquatics Federation (NAqF) as dem President, Mr. Chamberlain Nnamdi Dunkwu, pass away on Saturday. For lette...

item.title: Aga Khan University Surgery Residency Programme Becomes First in Kenya to Achieve Global Accreditation
item.description: The Surgery Residency Programme at Aga Khan University (AKU) Medical College in East Africa has achieved a historic milestone by becoming the first in Kenya and the wider region to receive accreditati...

item.title: Democrats Secure 28 State Legislative Seat Flips Since Trump's Reelection
item.description: Since President Donald Trump's reelection and inauguration in January 2025 for his second term, Democrats have achieved significant gains in state legislatures across the United States. Over the past ...

item.title: Community Bridge in Soshanguve Destroyed by Floods, Leaving Residents Stranded
item.description: In early March 2026, heavy rains in Gauteng caused severe flooding in Soshanguve, washing away a makeshift bridge that local residents had constructed to help children safely cross a sewage-contaminat...

item.title: Netanyahu Addresses Israeli Success in Repelling Recent Iranian and Hezbollah Attacks
item.description: On March 12, 2026, Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu held his first press conference since the conflict with Iran began on February 28. Speaking from Sderot, Netanyahu appeared confident and e...

item.title: Toronto man convicted of second-degree murder despite mental health issues
item.description: In a case that has drawn attention to the intersection of mental health and criminal responsibility, Toronto man Leahain Malcolm has been found guilty of second-degree murder for the 2021 stabbing of ...

item.title: PCSO Lotto Draws on March 12 End Without Jackpot Winners
item.description: The Philippine Charity Sweepstakes Office (PCSO) announced that no one won the jackpot prizes in the major lotto draws held on Thursday, March 12, 2026. In the Lotto 6/42 draw, no ticket matched the w...

item.title: Ikeja Residents Commend President Tinubu's Ramadan and Lent Food Support in Alausa Ward
item.description: Residents for Ikeja, Lagos State, don give big thanks to President Bola Tinubu for him Renewed Hope Agenda wey dey provide food palliatives during this Ramadan and Lenten fasting period. The Vice Chai...

item.title: Challenges in Home-Based Elder and Palliative Care in India
item.description: India is experiencing a rapidly ageing population, with older adults projected to make up nearly 20% of the population by 2050. National policies, such as the National Programme for Palliative Care, a...

item.title: US Air Force KC-135 Refuelling Plane Crashes in Western Iraq During Military Operations
item.description: A United States Air Force KC-135 refuelling aircraft has crashed in western Iraq amid ongoing US-Israel operations against Iran. The incident occurred in friendly airspace during Operation Epic Fury, ...

item.title: Nairobi Youths Disrupt Mortuary Over Flood Victim’s Body
item.description: In Nairobi, tensions ran high when a group of youths stormed the City Mortuary demanding the body of their friend, Nicholas Kyalo, who drowned in the deadly floods on 6 March 2026. The youths, citing ...

item.title: Married At First Sight expert Mel Schilling announces cancer has spread to her brain
item.description: Mel Schilling, the long-time relationship expert on Married At First Sight, has shared that her cancer has progressed and is now affecting her brain. First diagnosed with colon cancer in December 2023...

item.title: Reflecting on the Life and Legacy of King Goodwill Zwelithini
item.description: King Goodwill Zwelithini kaBhekuzulu, who passed away on 12 March 2021, remains a defining figure in the history of the Zulu nation. Ascending the throne in 1971 at the young age of 23, he became the ...

item.title: KFS and KEMRI Hold Talks on Collaborating for Plant-Based Medicines from Kenyan Forests
item.description: The Kenya Forest Service (KFS) and the Kenya Medical Research Institute (KEMRI) came together on Wednesday at KFS headquarters in Nairobi to discuss possible partnership. The main aim is to develop pl...

item.title: Iran Declares Continued Blockade of Strait of Hormuz Amid Rising Tensions
item.description: Iran’s newly appointed Supreme Leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, has stated that the country will continue to use the Strait of Hormuz as leverage against the United States and Israel. The comments, delivered...
